* explicit type declarations in tests

* using Cancellable Work Pattern recommandations

* events testing

* uicatalog url indicator + no crash Uri

* fix scenario test

* Link Osc8 test

* bad url is now has no effect and is o

Update Url property validation to retain previous value on invalid input.

* fix link test scheme color

* no url in cell  + SetNeedsDraw on url change + empty default url + update tests

* Refactor Link control: API, UI, and platform integration

- Added static Link.OpenUrl for platform-specific URL opening; removed OpenUrl from UICatalogRunnable.
- Link now handles mouse clicks and keyboard activation, opening URLs directly.
- Improved drawing: shows Url if Text is empty, disables link if Url is invalid, always clears Driver.CurrentUrl.
- Copy() now copies Text, not Url.
- SetUrl validates URLs; invalid URLs revert to DEFAULT_URL.
- IDesignable.EnableForDesign sets default Title and Text.
- Refactored Links scenario UI for clarity and usability; status bar now shows Link's Text.
- Updated all tests for new Link API and behaviors; improved test clarity.
- Minor code style improvements and use of System.Diagnostics for process launching.

* Refactor Link: decouple Text/Title/Url, expand tests

- IDesignable.EnableForDesign now sets Title and Url, not Text.
- Copy() now copies Url to clipboard instead of Text.
- Text, Title, and Url are now independent; setting one does not affect the others.
- TextFormatter displays Url if Text is empty; otherwise uses Text.
- DimAuto sizing uses Text width if set, otherwise Url, including wide chars.
- Url property now accepts any string (no URI validation).
- Setting the same Url does not fire change events.
- UrlChanging event can cancel Url changes.
- Rendering tests ensure invalid URLs do not produce OSC 8 hyperlinks and are styled as disabled.
- Tests updated and expanded to cover all new behaviors and edge cases.
- Minor test code cleanups and clarifications.

* Improve Link API documentation

Add comprehensive XML docs for the Link class covering the three
independent text properties (Text, Title, Url), OSC 8 hyperlink
rendering, draw-time URL validation, CWP event pattern, HotKey-to-next-peer
behavior, and Dim.Auto sizing. Enhance docs for all public members.

commit.message:
fix: eliminate CLS layout shifts from SPA activation and fixed elements (#1355) (#1357)

Root cause: SPA activation used display:none to hide SSR content, causing
a full layout recalculation (CLS 0.34-0.998). The VNext shell also lacked
the bm-content class, creating inconsistent min-height behaviour.

Fixes:
- site.css: Add .bm-ssr-hidden class (visibility:hidden + position:absolute)
  that removes content from visual flow without triggering layout shift.
  Add contain:layout style to .bm-navbar, .bm-content, .bm-footer to
  isolate paint/layout recalculations.
- vnext-app.js: SPA activation now uses classList.add('bm-ssr-hidden')
  instead of style.display='none'.
- RouteRegistrationExtensions.cs: VNext shell div#vnext-content now
  includes bm-content class and matching py-4 px-4 padding.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix S-003: Clean stale golang.org/x/sys entries from go.sum

Removed stale golang.org/x/sys v0.20.0 and v0.42.0 entries from go.sum.
These dependencies are not declared in go.mod and are not used by any
Go source code. Running 'go mod tidy' correctly cleaned them.

This addresses security finding S-003 (MEDIUM severity, Dependency
Hygiene category) from AUDIT.md Phase 1: Critical Security Fixes.

commit.message:
fix(agents): add bash tool to agent definitions

Ensure ado-kql-analyst, ado-scrum-helper, and ado-test-runner agents have access to the bash tool module. These agents require bash to execute their core workflows (az CLI commands for KQL queries, scrum standups, and test runs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Switch to 8 columns to match reference game layout

Reference screenshots (8×11 grid, 75px cells) confirmed 8 columns
is the canonical layout. With 8 cols the 1-9 sequence wraps with
a natural offset — row 1 starts with 9 1 2 3... instead of
repeating the same values as row 0.

This creates real strategic choices at game start: the 3 initial
valid pairs (1+9 vertical col0, 9+1 flat row-wrap, 1+9 vertical
col1) share cells, so order matters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(nexkit-file-watcher): add file watcher service for .nexkit directory protection

Implements NexkitFileWatcherService that monitors the .nexkit/ directory for
external file changes. When a user manually modifies a managed file, the
service rolls back the change and prompts for confirmation. Changes made
internally by Nexkit are suppressed to avoid false positives.

Features:
- FileSystemWatcher for .nexkit/**/* with content cache for rollback
- Per-path suppression (suppressPath) for individual file operations
- Bulk suppression (beginBulkOperation/endBulkOperation) for batch operations
- Rollback + user confirmation on file change/delete
- Warning on manual file creation in managed directory
- Singleton pattern for easy access from any service

Integration:
- TemplateFileOperations: suppress before install/uninstall
- BackupService: bulk suppress during delete/restore
- NexkitFileMigrationService: bulk suppress during migration
- ServiceContainer: registered and disposed
- extension.ts: watcher started on activation

Co-authored-by: decarufe <1083536+decarufe@users.noreply.github.com>

Add tests for singleton pattern, suppression methods, bulk operations,

Also update serviceContainer.test.ts to verify the new service.

commit.message:
fix: relaunch.sh — prefer ~/.dotnet SDK and skip Xcode version check (#324)

## Problem

`relaunch.sh` fails on machines where:

1. **.NET 10 SDK is installed via `dotnet-install.sh`** — installs to
`~/.dotnet/`, but the system `dotnet` at `/usr/local/share/dotnet/` is
an older version (e.g. 7.0). The script's bare `dotnet build` picks the
wrong one.

2. **Xcode is a minor version ahead** of what the .NET MacCatalyst SDK
expects (e.g. Xcode 26.3 vs expected 26.2), causing the build to fail
with a version mismatch error.

## Fix

- **PATH**: Prepend `~/.dotnet` to PATH if `~/.dotnet/dotnet` exists.
Conditional — no-op on machines with a single system-wide install.
- **Xcode**: Pass `-p:ValidateXcodeVersion=false` to `dotnet build`.
Apple ships Xcode updates faster than .NET SDK updates, so this mismatch
is common.

---------

commit.message:
Detect self-contained projects in DotNetComponentDetector (#1689)

* Detect self-contained projects in DotNetComponentDetector

Report whether a .NET project target is self-contained by appending -selfcontained to the projectType (e.g. application-selfcontained).

Uses a heuristic based on the assets file: for each target framework, if any PackageDownload name starts with a FrameworkReference name followed by .Runtime, the target is considered self-contained. This covers both SelfContained=true and PublishAot=true scenarios, as both result in runtime package downloads in the assets file.

This relationship is not a guarantee.  The actual relationship is defined by the SDK's KnownFramework items, but we can't read those from build assets.  This convention has been followed for all in-support framework versions and should be acceptable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Scale difficulty via classic sequence repetition (option 2)

Replace snake-generated random boards with four classic-sequence
variants of increasing length. The 1-9 repeating sequence creates
genuine forced dependencies — clearing one number unlocks others
rather than every pair being a direct neighbor.

Variants (all at 6 columns wide):
  Intro    1-9 x 2  = 18 cells,  3 initial valid pairs
  Standard 1-9 x 4  = 36 cells,  7 initial valid pairs
  Hard     1-9 x 6  = 54 cells, 11 initial valid pairs
  Expert   1-9 x 8  = 72 cells, 15 initial valid pairs

Web Worker, activeWorker, genStatus/genAttempt UI elements.
Board creation is now instant and synchronous.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix TimeEditor cursor/insertion by normalizing time format patterns

Single-digit format specifiers (h, H, m, s) caused variable-width
fields where cursor positions shifted based on the current time value.
Typing "12" at position 0 of a single-digit hour would place "1" in
hours and "2" in minutes because CursorRight skipped the separator
at position 1.

Normalize all format specifiers to 2-digit versions (hh, HH, mm, ss)
so field positions are always consistent. Remove the leading space
hack from DisplayText since padding is no longer needed.

Add 9 unit tests covering cursor navigation, insertion at all digit
positions, separator skipping, and DriverAssert rendering verification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Improve TimeEditor width and TextValidateField cursor logic

- TimeEditor now sets width to fit full DisplayText (incl. AM/PM).
- TextValidateField allows cursor one past last editable char.
- Fixed width calculation for fixed fields (DisplayText.Length + 1).
- Updated tests for new width and cursor behavior.
- Added labels in TimeAndDate demo to show time patterns.
- Added tests to verify full AM/PM visibility and width logic.
- Improves usability and prevents clipping of time strings.

* Improve cursor handling at end of masked text fields

Enhances navigation and editing in masked fields (TextValidateField, TimeEditor) to prevent the cursor from moving backward when pressing right arrow from the blank cell past the last editable character. Backspace from the blank cell now deletes the last editable character. TimeEditor minimum width is increased by one to always allow a blank cell. Updates logic for right-arrow navigation in fixed providers. Adds unit tests to verify correct cursor and editing behavior at the end of input. These changes address subtle usability issues and make cursor movement more intuitive.

* Replace TimeField with new TimeEditor control

Removed the TimeField control, its tests, and documentation. Introduced TimeEditor as the new time input control, based on TextValidateField with culture-aware formatting. Updated all references, scenarios, and docs to use TimeEditor instead of TimeField. TimeField source and tests have been deleted.

* Refactor TimeEditor and providers, remove VerifyChar method

Refactored TimeEditor, TimeTextProvider, NetMaskedTextProvider, and TextRegexProvider for clarity and consistency. Removed the VerifyChar method from ITextValidateProvider and all implementations, simplifying the provider interface. Standardized event raising and improved handling of AM/PM, manual parsing, and pattern normalization in TimeTextProvider. Updated TextValidateField drawing and key handling logic for simplicity. Modernized and clarified all related tests to match the new API and style. Made various code style improvements and updated documentation throughout.

* Implement CWP and IValue<T> for TextValidateField/TimeEditor

Refactored TextValidateField and TimeEditor to fully implement the IValue<T> interface and follow the Cancellable Work Pattern (CWP) for value changes.
- TextValidateField now exposes Value, ValueChanging, and ValueChanged events, and synchronizes value changes from both programmatic and user input, with support for cancellation and reversion.
- Introduced SuppressValueEvents to prevent unwanted event recursion.
- TimeEditor now uses a private _value field, leverages CWPPropertyHelper for value changes, and raises strongly-typed TimeSpan events.
- Removed manual event wiring and last-known-value tracking in favor of the new base class pattern.
- These changes improve consistency, extensibility, and support for data binding and undo/redo scenarios.

* Replace DateField with new DateEditor based on TextValidateField

Add DateTextProvider (ITextValidateProvider for dates with culture-aware
formatting, pattern normalization, separator handling) and DateEditor
(extends TextValidateField, implements IValue<DateTime?> with CWP).
Remove legacy DateField and update all usages in DatePicker, scenarios,
docs, and indexes. Full test coverage in DateEditorTests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactored event raising in Date, Time, Masked, and Regex text providers by introducing a private RaiseTextChanged method. OnTextChanged is now virtual and serves as a subclass hook, improving extensibility. Updated all event triggers to use RaiseTextChanged. Improved null safety in TextRegexProvider by initializing _text to an empty list. Fixed CleanSeparator logic in DateTextProvider for short separators. commit.message:
docs(*): add bootstrap step to `copilot-instructions.md` (#517)

The Copilot instructions did not tell agents to bootstrap the workspace
before running validation or making package changes. In a fresh clone,
that leads to avoidable failures and wasted setup time.

- **Summary**
- Add explicit bootstrap guidance to `.github/copilot-instructions.md`.
- Place it directly before the existing test/build/lint workflow so the
install requirement is seen before any validation commands.

- **Changes**
  - Introduce a new `Bootstrapping` section in the Copilot instructions.
- Require `npm install` from the repo root before tests, builds, lint,
or implementation work.
- Clarify that work should not start until install completes
successfully because workspace dependencies are shared across the
monorepo.

- **Resulting guidance**
  ```md
  ## Bootstrapping

- In a fresh clone, run `npm install` from the repo root before
attempting tests, builds, lint, or package changes.
- Do not start validation or implementation work until the install has
completed successfully, because the workspace dependencies are required
across the monorepo.
  ```

commit.message:
feat: add two-tier group isolation to all read methods in async_neo4j_service

All READ methods now accept an optional `group_ids: List[str]` parameter
and compute `effective_ids = group_ids or [group_id, "__global__"]`.

MATCH-based queries: changed `= $group_id` to `IN $effective_ids` and
pass `effective_ids=effective_ids` in params.

VECTOR INDEX SEARCH queries: converted to UNION ALL pattern querying
both `$group_id` and `$global_group_id="__global__"` separately (vector
index WHERE clause only supports single equality).

PPR builder methods (_build_ppr_query_entity_only,
_build_ppr_query_with_section_graph, _build_ppr_query_weighted_section_graph):
all inline `group_id` Cypher variables renamed to `effective_ids` and
all `= group_id` changed to `IN effective_ids`.

Updated methods:
- get_entities_by_importance
- get_entities_by_names (4 sub-queries)
- get_entities_by_vector_similarity (UNION ALL)
- expand_neighbors
- get_entity_relationships
- personalized_pagerank_native
- personalized_pagerank_weighted
- get_community_peers
- get_entities_by_sentence_ids
- get_chunks_for_entities
- check_field_exists_in_document
- check_field_pattern_in_document
- check_pattern_in_docs_by_keyword (2 sub-queries)
- semantic_multihop_beam (3 hop queries + UNION ALL vector expansion)
- get_entity_document_coverage

Backward compatible: all existing callers pass only `group_id`; the new
`group_ids` parameter defaults to None.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Switch to Flex Consumption plan with blob-based deployment

Following the official MS quickstart template for Flex Consumption
with managed identities:
- Flex Consumption plan (FC1) uses blob container for deployment
  instead of Azure Files — no file share or shared key access needed
- Storage account: allowSharedKeyAccess=false (policy-compliant)
- User-assigned managed identity with Blob Data Owner/Contributor,
  Queue Data Contributor, Table Data Contributor roles
- functionAppConfig with blobContainer deployment storage
- App settings use AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName/credential/clientId
- No WEBSITE_CONTENTAZUREFILECONNECTIONSTRING needed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add SAFETY comments to all unsafe blocks in Rust code

Step 11: Document safety invariants for all 10 unsafe blocks across 4 files:
- allocator.rs: 7 blocks (mmap/munmap, slice conversions)
- drm.rs: 1 block (ioctl wrapper)
- pm4.rs: 1 block (buffer reinterpretation)
- xe.rs: 1 block (DRM version query)

Each SAFETY comment explains:
- Required invariants (valid pointers, fds, lifetimes)
- Memory safety guarantees
- Alignment and initialization requirements
- Aliasing constraints

Validation: All 10 blocks documented, cargo build succeeds
Zero regressions: Rust-only changes, no Go code modified

commit.message:
fix: open markdown preview on mobile plan file click

On mobile, clicking a plan file in the Plans tab was dispatching
OPEN_DIALOG (the task creation dialog) instead of opening the
markdown preview. Fix by dispatching coc-open-markdown-review
on single click on mobile, consistent with double-click on desktop.

Also removes now-unused useQueue import/usage from TaskTree.
